executive_summary: Canada is experiencing a massive coordinated ransomware campaign affecting over 100 organizations in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Eastern Townships School Board, Peterborough Public Health, and National Money Mart (with confirmed database leak). The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 20+ victims, followed by Play, Clop, and SafePay gangs. Critical infrastructure including healthcare, education, public sector, and financial services are under sustained assault across all provinces.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 100+ victims across all provinces and sectors in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure heavily impacted: healthcare facilities (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services, Bison Family Medical), education (Eastern Townships School Board, College Superieur De Montreal, Teccart), public sector (Peterborough Public Health, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Municipality of North Perth, Thunder Bay Counselling), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network). No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose targeted by two separate ransomware groups (Coinbase Cartel and Shiny Hunters), Active Green + Ross, Kids & Company
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company - database leaked by Everest ransomware group, Morison Insurance Brokers, Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, SMT Lawyers

report_2w.executive_summary: Canada's cybersecurity agency (CCCS) has issued alerts for two critical vulnerabilities: CVE-2026-35616 in Fortinet FortiClientEMS and CVE-2026-3055 in Citrix NetScaler products. The Ivanti EPMM flaw (CVE-2026-1340) is being actively exploited in ongoing attacks, prompting CISA warnings. Multiple phishing campaigns are leveraging Google Storage to deliver remote access trojans, while F5 BIG-IP and Cisco products face critical security flaws. Adobe Reader has a zero-day vulnerability exploited since December via malicious PDFs, and Chrome's fourth zero-day of 2026 (CVE-2026-5281) requires federal patching by April 15.

items.description: Vulnerability impacting Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway
items.software_affected: Citrix NetScaler ADC and NetScaler Gateway

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued two official alerts this week: AL26-007 for Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616) and AL26-006 for Citrix NetScaler products (CVE-2026-3055). No direct attacks on Canadian critical infrastructure were reported, though healthcare organizations using NetScaler are flagged as at-risk for ransomware exploitation. No specific incidents involving Canadian retail or financial institutions were identified in the threat feeds.
